Title: Chloroplast Sec14-like 1 (CPSFL1) is essential for normal chloroplast development and affects carotenoid accumulation in Chlamydomonas
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: 4A+ (mutant generated in this background); complemented/backcross lines used
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: photoprotection; accessory light harvesting; essential for assembly, stabilization, and repair of the photosynthetic apparatus
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: chloroplast envelope; thylakoid membranes; plastoglobules; eyespot
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes carotenoid accumulation and phytoene formation/flux; loss of CPSFL1 lowers total carotenoids and especially β-carotene, impairs carotenoid-rich chloroplast structures, and causes extreme light sensitivity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: insertional mutant analysis; genetic cosegregation; complementation with CPSFL1 cDNA/genomic DNA; immunoblotting; HPLC pigment profiling; norflurazon-induced phytoene accumulation assay; carotenoid-binding assay in E. coli; lipid-binding assay; FIB-SEM/TEM ultrastructure
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPSFL1 encodes a chloroplast Sec14-like CRAL-TRIO protein required for photoautotrophic growth and normal chloroplast development. 2. Loss of CPSFL1 decreases total carotenoids and strongly reduces β-carotene and phytoene accumulation capacity, indicating impaired flux before or at phytoene synthesis. 3. CPSFL1 binds phytoene and β-carotene in heterologous assays and binds phosphatidic acid in vitro, supporting a role in carotenoid synthesis/transport within chloroplasts.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Sec14-like CRAL-TRIO hydrophobic ligand-binding protein; N-terminal chloroplast transit peptide; N-CRAL-TRIO and CRAL-TRIO domains
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast; both soluble and membrane-associated fractions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein detected in whole-cell soluble and membrane fractions; functional in chloroplasts of dark-grown and light-shifted cells
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark growth; low light shift; high light; norflurazon treatment; acetate-containing mixotrophic conditions
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes nonphotosynthetic phenotype, photoinhibition susceptibility, photobleaching, defective PSII macroorganization, and failure to grow under light above very low irradiance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve chloroplast carotenoid accumulation and carotenoid-rich structure formation, but essentiality and severe chloroplast-development defects upon loss suggest that only precise upregulation or allele engineering, not knockout, would be suitable
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study concludes CPSFL1 is not a core carotenoid biosynthetic enzyme but an auxiliary factor proposed to regulate phytoene synthesis/flux and/or carotenoid transport. Arabidopsis ortholog is At5g63060. Dark-grown cpsfl1 does not show major chloroplast glycerolipid defects despite PA binding.
